name: kartikeya/sk
shortDescription: Kartikeya, also known as Skanda, is the Hindu god of war and the commander-in-chief of the army of the gods. He is the son of Shiva and Parvati and is revered for his courage, wisdom, and leadership.
description: Kartikeya, also known as Skanda, Murugan, Subramanya, and Kumara, is a prominent Hindu deity, primarily revered in South India and parts of North India. He is the god of war, the commander-in-chief of the Devas (gods), and represents courage, youth, and power. His birth story varies across different Puranas, but the most common narrative depicts him as the son of Shiva and Parvati, created to defeat the demon Taraka. Some accounts describe him as being born from Shiva's seed, carried by Agni (fire god) and then by the Ganges river, before finally being born from the Krittika sisters (Pleiades). He is often depicted as a handsome young man, riding a peacock (Paravani), holding a spear (Vel), and sometimes accompanied by his consorts, Devasena and Valli. Kartikeya is celebrated for his wisdom, strategic prowess, and his role in vanquishing evil forces. He is a symbol of martial prowess and divine leadership, embodying the qualities necessary to overcome adversity. Temples dedicated to Kartikeya are found throughout India, with significant shrines in Tamil Nadu, where he is known as Murugan, and is deeply integrated into the cultural and religious fabric. His worship is associated with fertility, courage, and victory over negative forces, making him a beloved and important deity in the Hindu pantheon.
